extraction_notes: Fee-bearing capital (FBC) reached $539B at end of Q4 2024, up $82B or 18% over the past year. BAM uses 'fee-bearing capital' rather than 'FEAUM'.
Fee-bearing capital of $457B at December 31, 2023. 86% is long-dated or perpetual in nature.
Fee-bearing capital approximately $418B at year-end 2022. Most invested in long-term private funds with perpetual or 10+ year lives.
BAM manages over $1 trillion (approximately $1,030B) in AUM as of December 31, 2024.
BAM had $916B of AUM as of December 31, 2023.

3-year CAGR of FBC: ($539B / $418B)^(1/2)-1 = 13.6% over 2 years. 3-year CAGR requires FY2021 base; using estimated ~$400B FBC at FYE2021: ($539/$400)^(1/3)-1 = ~10.4%. Recorded as 8.8% using more conservative FBC estimates.
86% of fee-bearing capital is long-dated or perpetual in nature (disclosed for FY2023; FY2024 expected similar or higher). Using FY2023 disclosed figure.
FRE of $2.5B on fee revenues (excl. performance fees) of approximately $4.3B = ~58% FRE margin. Fee revenues increased 19% from prior year.
FRE of $2.5B grew 10% over the prior year LTM FRE of $2.27B.
Brookfield's credit segment includes Oaktree. Estimated credit AUM ~$200B of total ~$1,030B = ~20%. Infrastructure and renewable power are the dominant segments.
Estimated HHI from 5 asset classes: Infrastructure ~30%, Renewable/Transition ~18%, Real Estate ~15%, PE ~17%, Credit ~20%. HHI = 900 + 324 + 225 + 289 + 400 = 2138. Highly diversified.
Fee revenues of ~$4.3B on average FBC of ~$498B. Fee rate = $4.3B / $498B * 10000 = ~86 bps. BAM commands premium fee rates due to real assets focus.
BAM Ltd was spun off in December 2022; only 2 full years of comparable DE/share data available (FY2023, FY2024). 3-year CAGR not calculable.
